risks.description: Material is not legible in the feature graph. Routing assumes a machinable automotive casting alloy; cutting data, coating compatibility, foundry process, and any stress-relief/aging cycle must be confirmed.
risks.mitigation: Confirm title-block material and casting/heat-treatment condition before releasing process plan.

risks.description: Several formula inputs are not determinable from the feature graph, especially M10 recess diameter/thread depth, central bore depths, clevis hole local wall thicknesses, and clevis slot length.
risks.mitigation: Use the 3D model and verified drawing revision to fill missing feature depths and counterbore diameters before NC programming.

risks.description: Cored bore starting diameters and as-cast machining allowance distribution are not provided; rough/finish boring inputs use final feature sizes but leave bore_start_diameter_mm null where unknown.
risks.mitigation: Obtain casting model, foundry drawing, and first-article stock-map before finalizing roughing toolpaths.

risks.description: The shop profile has in-house inspection but no in-house CMM. Several position/profile controls may require external CMM for launch/FAI even if production gauges are built in-house.
risks.mitigation: Plan external CMM FAI or develop dedicated checking fixture/gauge package through the tool room.

risks.description: The drawing note appears to specify Zn-Ni coating on insert nuts, not necessarily the housing. The exact coating specification and thickness are not available.
risks.mitigation: Confirm whether inserts are purchased pre-coated, coated in-house, or coated by an approved Zn-Ni vendor; confirm thread gauge requirement after coating.

outside_processes_needed.reason: Almity profile does not list foundry casting. The complex housing geometry, ribs, pockets, bosses, and quantity 1000 justify a near-net casting supplied by a foundry before machining.
CMM is not listed as in-house; GD&T position/profile controls likely need external CMM for first-article or PPAP/sample validation.
outside_processes_needed.sequence_position: after final assembly/cleaning for FAI or sample inspection

feasibility.assessment_notes: Feasibility is acceptable under the provided instruction. The 335.78 × 232.62 × 143.6 mm envelope fits the listed VMC travel. Tolerances around ±0.05 mm and GD&T 0.15–0.3 mm are achievable with VMC/SPM machining, reaming/boring, dedicated fixtures, and appropriate inspection. Plan is provisional until material, coating scope, and missing depths are confirmed.

stock.material: Unknown cast alloy; provisional routing assumes a machinable automotive casting alloy per confirmed drawing/model material.

starting_dimensions.notes: Near-net casting envelope estimated as finished envelope plus about 3 mm machining allowance per side on controlled surfaces. Actual casting stock map must come from foundry/tooling drawing.

stock.why: The feature graph describes a complex cast housing with ribs, lightening pockets/windows, bosses, clevis lugs, and an annular central housing. At 1000 pieces, machining this from block would waste excessive material; the casting route sequence also states that castings skip saw-cut stock and begin by establishing machining datums on the pre-formed blank.

evidence.cited_text: Complex cast housing body with radial ribs, irregular pockets/windows, mounting bosses and clevis/lug geometry.
Envelope 335.78 x 232.62 x 143.6 mm; envelope_shape complex_3d.
Applicable when part geometry has variable wall thickness, ribs, bosses, and organic shapes; first machining establishes datums on as-cast surfaces.

route.route_name: Near-net casting + VMC/SPM machining + coated insert installation route for CM00B337200-1 HOUSING
route.route_reason: Use an outsourced near-net casting for the complex ribbed housing, then rough-machine casting skin, establish and finish datum A, finish datum bores B/C/D and the central bore stack on VMC, finish clevis/perimeter features on VMC, use SPM for the repeated M10 insert-nut recess pattern, then deburr/clean/mark, coat or receive Zn-Ni insert nuts, install inserts, test, and inspect. CNC lathe was not selected because the part is not primarily rotational and cannot be economically held as a turning workpiece despite having coaxial bore features.

setups.machine_reason: No foundry equipment is listed in the shop inventory. A near-net casting is required by the complex housing geometry and production quantity.

workholding.grip_description: Pattern, mold/die, cores, gating, risers and trim fixtures supplied by foundry.
workholding.fixture_notes: Casting tooling must include shrinkage, draft, core prints, and machining allowance on datum pads, bores, bosses, and clevis features.

setups.stock_state_before: Approved material specification and foundry/casting tooling release pending material confirmation.
setups.stock_state_after: Cleaned, trimmed, stress-relieved or aged near-net housing casting with machining allowance and foundry documentation.

sub_operations.operation_name: Produce near-net cast housing blank with ribs, pockets, bosses, annular body, clevis lugs and machining stock
sub_operations.reason: The geometry is complex and near-net casting minimizes material waste at 1000 pieces.
sub_operations.notes: Casting material, method, shrinkage allowance and actual machining stock map must be confirmed.

sub_operations.operation_name: Trim gates/risers, shot-blast, remove flash, and stress-relieve or age casting per material specification
sub_operations.reason: Casting route requires cleaning and stabilization before machining so residual stress and cast skin do not distort finish features.
sub_operations.notes: Stress-relief/aging cycle is conditional on confirmed alloy; required if specified by foundry/material standard.

setups.outside_reason: Foundry casting capability is not listed in the shop profile.
setups.inspection_at_setup: Foundry dimensional check, visual flash/porosity check, material certificate, and heat-treatment/stress-relief certificate if applicable.
setups.setup_reason: Start from a near-net casting because the housing is complex, ribbed, pocketed, and high-volume.

setups.setup_name: Rough machine provisional datum A pads and support faces
setups.machine_reason: VMC is the correct first in-house machine because the casting is complex/prismatic with +Z datum pads and controlled support faces; the part fits 600 x 400 x 400 mm travel.

workholding.method: custom casting nest with 3-2-1 rough location
workholding.grip_description: Locate on robust as-cast bosses and support pads; clamp on noncritical cast ribs/bosses while leaving A1-A4 pad areas accessible.
workholding.fixture_notes: Tool room should provide a nest that references stable as-cast features and allows first cuts below casting skin.

setups.stock_state_before: Cleaned near-net casting with as-cast surfaces and allowance.
setups.stock_state_after: Casting with rough provisional datum A pad set and support faces cleaned below cast skin; finish stock left for final datum machining.

sub_operations.operation_name: Rough face mill 4× Ø40 datum target pads A1-A4 and adjacent support faces below cast skin
sub_operations.reason: Casting and milling rules require datum surfaces first and first cut below cast skin; leave stock for later final datum pass after roughing other features.

input_sources.value: 2.0 minimum first cut below casting skin

tool.tool_description: Indexable face mill for cast skin roughing
tool.notes: Use inserts suitable for interrupted casting cuts.

sub_operations.notes: Final flatness/profile pass is delayed until after rough machining to reduce casting distortion risk.

sub_operations.operation_name: Inspect rough datum A cleanup and remaining machining stock distribution
sub_operations.reason: Before roughing all other features, confirm there is adequate stock on the pads and controlled faces.

tool.tool_description: Height gauge/indicator and visual stock check

setups.inspection_at_setup: Check rough A pad cleanup and stock remaining before roughing other features.
setups.setup_reason: Establish a provisional primary datum plane without final-finishing it before major roughing.

setups.setup_name: VMC rough all machined features from provisional datum A
setups.machine_reason: VMC supports mixed rough boring, drilling, slotting, pocket/profile cleanup and contouring on the complex housing in a controlled fixture.

workholding.method: custom fixture on provisional A pads with side/rest supports
workholding.grip_description: Rest on rough A pads and robust cast supports; clamp away from bores, clevis slots and mounting bosses.
workholding.fixture_notes: Allow cutter clearance for +Z roughing of central bore, clevis slots, perimeter edges, and M10 bosses.

setups.stock_state_before: Casting with rough provisional A pad set.
setups.stock_state_after: All major machined features roughed/opened with finish stock left on datum pads, precision bores, clevis faces, profile walls and insert recesses.

sub_operations.operation_name: Spot rough hole locations for B/C bores, central bore, clevis holes and four M10 insert positions
sub_operations.reason: Hole-making rule requires spotting before twist/peck drilling to reduce drill wander on cast surfaces.

tool.tool_description: Carbide spot drill, 90° or 120° point
tool.notes: One spotting tool used across rough hole families.

sub_operations.notes: Grouped spotting uses the largest feature diameter for formula reference; NC program will spot each hole location appropriately.

sub_operations.operation_name: Rough peck drill/open B/C datum-bores and clevis/lug holes leaving finish stock
sub_operations.reason: Depths exceed 3× diameter for several extracted holes; peck drilling is required for chip evacuation before later final drill/ream/boring.

input_sources.value: 7 grouped B/C and clevis rough holes

tool.tool_description: Through-coolant or peck-cycle drills sized per rough hole family
tool.notes: Actual rough drill diameters to leave stock for final reaming/boring are to be defined in CAM.

sub_operations.notes: Final drilling and reaming occur later in the same finish setups; this rough pass is only for stress/stock relief.

sub_operations.operation_name: Rough bore/interpolate central datum D bore and coaxial central bore train
sub_operations.reason: Open the large cored/solid central features and leave uniform stock for finish boring after datum A is finished.

tool.tool_description: Rough boring head or circular interpolation end mill
tool.notes: Cored starting diameter is unknown.

sub_operations.notes: bore_start_diameter_mm and bore depth are not determinable from the extracted graph.

sub_operations.operation_name: Rough mill clevis fork slots and lug face stock
sub_operations.reason: Remove bulk material from clevis openings before finish slots, faces, and hole sizing.

tool.tool_description: Carbide roughing end mill for slot opening
tool.notes: Use adaptive roughing and leave finish stock on slot walls.

sub_operations.notes: slot_length_mm is not available in the feature graph.

sub_operations.operation_name: Rough profile clean-up on exterior perimeter, annular cast bosses, ribs/pocket edges where controlled by model
sub_operations.reason: Rough controlled cast profiles after primary roughing and before final datum/finish operations; as-cast relief ribs/pockets are not fully machined unless model requires cleanup.

input_sources.value: 232.62 available perimeter span reference, not full perimeter

tool.tool_description: End mill for profile roughing and cast cleanup
tool.notes: Use 3D model for actual perimeter/pocket path.

sub_operations.notes: Exact pocket/rib boundaries are model-controlled and not fully dimensioned in the raster drawing.

sub_operations.operation_name: Rough drill four M10 insert/nut pilot locations
sub_operations.reason: Open repeated mounting-boss locations after rough spotting; final counterbore/recess sizing occurs later on SPM after datum finish.

tool.tool_description: Pilot/rough drill for M10 insert location
tool.notes: Actual drilled diameter depends on confirmed insert-nut design.

sub_operations.notes: M10 thread depth and insert-hole specification are not confirmed; this operation is preparatory only.

setups.inspection_at_setup: Verify rough feature cleanup and remaining finish stock after major roughing.
setups.setup_reason: Rough all major cast features before final datum/feature finishing to satisfy casting distortion control rules.

setups.setup_name: Finish datum A pad set and controlled support faces
setups.machine_reason: VMC face milling provides controlled flatness/profile on the four datum target pads and related support surfaces after rough stresses are relieved.

workholding.method: fixture on rough A and cast supports
workholding.grip_description: Support on rough pads and stable underside casting regions; clamp off the finished pad surfaces as much as practical.
workholding.fixture_notes: Fixture must not distort the four A1-A4 datum pads during final light passes.

setups.stock_state_before: Rough-machined casting with all major features opened and finish stock remaining.
setups.stock_state_after: Final datum A pad pattern and controlled support surfaces established for all downstream datum-referenced finish operations.

sub_operations.operation_name: Finish face mill 4× Ø40 datum target pads and controlled support surfaces to flatness/profile requirement
sub_operations.reason: Datum A must be final before finishing B/C/D bores, M10 pattern, clevis holes and profile surfaces referenced to A/B/C.

tool.tool_description: Wiper-insert face mill for datum finish pass
tool.notes: Use light climb finish pass.

sub_operations.notes: axial_depth_mm for final cleanup is not specified; set during NC based on measured remaining stock.

sub_operations.operation_name: Inspect final datum A pad pattern, common-zone flatness and profile relationship
sub_operations.reason: Downstream features reference datum A, so final A must be accepted before machining B/C/D and hole patterns.

tool.tool_description: Indicator/height gauge or fixture gauge for datum target plane

setups.inspection_at_setup: Mandatory in-process acceptance of datum A before finish machining datum-referenced features.
setups.setup_reason: Final datum A is the primary datum for B/C/D bores, M10 pattern, clevis holes and perimeter profile controls.

setups.setup_name: Finish datum B/C bores and central D/coaxial bore stack
setups.machine_reason: VMC interpolation boring and reaming can hold the datum hole positions relative to final datum A in one fixture; lathe is rejected because the whole housing is not rotational or chuckable.

workholding.method: precision fixture on final datum A with rough side locators
workholding.grip_description: Rest on final A pad targets; clamp on noncritical bosses; use fixture bushings/probing to locate rough B/C openings before finish drilling/reaming.
workholding.fixture_notes: Consider hardened locating pads and removable clamps to keep B/C/D features accessible.

setups.stock_state_before: Rough-machined casting with final datum A established.
setups.stock_state_after: Datum B/C bores, datum D bore, and central coaxial bore/step features finished and inspected.

sub_operations.operation_name: Final drill/size-prep 2× Ø16.90 datum bores B and C for reaming
sub_operations.reason: Final ream-size preparation is done in the same setup immediately before reaming to preserve position and concentricity.

tool.tool_description: Long series drill or ream-prep drill with peck cycle
tool.notes: Actual pre-ream drill diameter selected by toolroom/CAM.

sub_operations.notes: Depth uses extracted through depth; confirm local wall thickness.

sub_operations.operation_name: Ream 2× Ø16.90 datum bores B and C to 16.85–16.95 mm limits
sub_operations.reason: Reaming immediately after final drill-prep in the same setup satisfies hole-making rules and achieves datum bore size/finish.

tool.tool_description: Ø16.90 machine reamer
tool.notes: Use bore gauge/pin gauge after reaming.

sub_operations.operation_name: Finish bore Ø72.04 datum D and Ø70.00 central stepped bore surfaces
sub_operations.reason: Datum D position references A/B/C and should be finished after A and B/C are established.

tool.tool_description: Adjustable fine boring head for datum D bore
tool.notes: Use 3D model for bore depth/step locations.

sub_operations.notes: hole_depth_mm and bore_start_diameter_mm are not extracted; clarify before cycle-time calculation.

sub_operations.operation_name: Finish circular-interpolate coaxial Ø74, Ø82, Ø90.02 steps, annular profile, draft and R3 relief surfaces
sub_operations.reason: These features are coaxial to datum D/central bore and should be finished after datum D is established.

tool.tool_description: End mill/ball mill for circular step, taper and R3 relief finishing
tool.notes: Use CAM model for axial step depths and draft surfaces.

sub_operations.notes: Exact axial depths and meaning of O/R, I/P and draft notes require clarification.

sub_operations.operation_name: Inspect datum B/C/D bore sizes and coaxial central feature relationship
sub_operations.reason: B/C/D are datum features for downstream geometry and final inspection.

tool.tool_description: Bore gauge, pin gauges and fixture datum check
tool.notes: CMM may be needed for FAI.

setups.inspection_at_setup: Bore gauges/pins for B/C/D; verify central coaxial features before moving to clevis finishing.
setups.setup_reason: Finish critical datums and central geometry after final A is established.

setups.setup_name: Finish clevis slots, lug holes, stepped walls and controlled perimeter
setups.machine_reason: The clevis slots, lug faces, perimeter profile and multi-size hole features require VMC milling, boring, reaming and contouring from datum A/B/C/D.

workholding.method: dedicated fixture on datum A pads with B/C locating pins
workholding.grip_description: Locate on A pads and hardened pins in B/C bores; support near clevis lugs and central housing; leave clevis openings and perimeter accessible.
workholding.fixture_notes: Backside deburr access must be planned for through holes and slots.

setups.stock_state_before: Housing with final A, B, C, D and central bore/step features.
setups.stock_state_after: Clevis slot/face/hole set, stepped walls, exterior perimeter profile, local chamfers and related controlled surfaces finished.

sub_operations.operation_name: Finish mill 2× clevis fork slots to 55.20 mm and finish inner/outer lug faces
sub_operations.reason: Slots and faces are milled before final clevis holes to avoid later distortion and to establish clean lug wall geometry.

tool.tool_description: Carbide finish end mill for slot walls/lug faces
tool.notes: Use climb finishing on slot walls.

sub_operations.notes: slot_length_mm not extracted; 3D model required.

sub_operations.operation_name: Finish stepped clevis wall stack and shelves per 3.00, 8.50, 11.50 and 15.00 mm dimensions
sub_operations.reason: Stepped wall geometry is finished after rough slotting and before final hole sizing.

tool.tool_description: Carbide end mill for wall shelves/steps
tool.notes: May require alternate +Y/-Y fixturing if side access is not possible from +Z.

sub_operations.operation_name: Finish bore/interpolate clevis/lug Ø30.60 bore
sub_operations.reason: The larger coaxial lug bore is finished before smaller coaxial holes to reduce drill deflection and establish the larger seat.

tool.tool_description: Adjustable boring head or circular interpolation end mill

sub_operations.operation_name: Final drill/size-prep 2× Ø20.10 clevis/lug hole set
sub_operations.reason: Prepare the smaller coaxial clevis holes immediately before reaming in the same setup.

tool.tool_description: Drill selected for ream-prep of Ø20.10 holes
tool.notes: Actual pre-ream diameter to be selected by CAM.

sub_operations.notes: hole_depth_mm/local wall thickness not extracted.

sub_operations.operation_name: Ream 2× Ø20.10 clevis/lug hole set to 20.00–20.20 mm limits
sub_operations.reason: Reaming immediately follows drill-prep in the same setup for size and positional consistency.

tool.tool_description: Ø20.10 machine reamer

sub_operations.notes: hole_depth_mm/local wall thickness not extracted.

sub_operations.operation_name: Final drill/size-prep 2× Ø10.50 clevis holes
sub_operations.reason: Prepare 2× Ø10.50 clevis holes before reaming; smaller holes are after larger intersecting/coaxial features.

tool.tool_description: Drill selected for Ø10.50 ream-prep
tool.notes: Actual pre-ream diameter to be selected by CAM.

sub_operations.notes: hole_depth_mm/local wall thickness not extracted.

sub_operations.operation_name: Ream 2× Ø10.50 clevis holes to 10.35–10.65 mm limits
sub_operations.reason: Ream after final drill-prep in same setup for consistent size and position.

tool.tool_description: Ø10.50 machine reamer

sub_operations.notes: hole_depth_mm/local wall thickness not extracted.

sub_operations.operation_name: Finish controlled exterior perimeter profile, R20.0 and R18.5 radii, and typical rib/wall profile references
sub_operations.reason: Exterior profile is referenced to A/B/C, so finish after datums B/C are complete.

tool.tool_description: Carbide end mill for profile/radius finish
tool.notes: Full perimeter path must come from 3D model.

sub_operations.notes: True profile length requires the 3D model; 232.62 is only an extracted span reference.

sub_operations.operation_name: Mill Detail Y local 2× 1.0 mm / 10° chamfers and reliefs
sub_operations.reason: Local edge reliefs are applied after surrounding boss/profile geometry is complete.

tool.tool_description: Small chamfer mill or form tool for 10° relief
tool.notes: Feature low-confidence; verify Detail Y.

setups.inspection_at_setup: Check clevis slot width, lug bore sizes, perimeter profile references and local Detail Y relief.
setups.setup_reason: Finish datum-referenced clevis and perimeter features after A/B/C/D are established.

setups.setup_name: SPM finish four M10 insert-nut recess pattern
setups.machine_reason: For quantity 1000, the repeated four-location M10 insert/nut pattern benefits from dedicated SPM drilling/counterboring fixture after datum A/B/C are established.

workholding.method: dedicated SPM fixture on A pads with B/C pins
workholding.grip_description: Locate on final A pads and B/C datum pins; clamp clear of four mounting bosses and Ø40 pad areas.
workholding.fixture_notes: Fixture must control hole pattern true position to A/B/C and keep counterbore/nut seats accessible.

setups.stock_state_before: Housing with final datum features and clevis/perimeter machining complete; M10 rough pilot locations present.
setups.stock_state_after: Four insert/nut counterbores/recesses and M10 locations finished for insert installation or direct-thread clarification.

sub_operations.operation_name: Spot 4× M10 insert/nut final locations
sub_operations.reason: Spotting before final drilling controls pattern position and prevents drill wander on boss surfaces.

tool.tool_description: SPM spot drill for four-boss pattern

sub_operations.operation_name: Final drill 4× M10 insert/nut holes per confirmed insert design
sub_operations.reason: Prepare holes/recess axes before counterboring; tapping is not planned unless clarified as direct threaded housing rather than insert nuts.

tool.tool_description: SPM drill sized to insert/hole specification
tool.notes: Diameter may change if insert nut OD requires a different pilot.

sub_operations.notes: thread_depth_mm and hole_depth_mm are not legible; counterbore/recess diameter is not legible.

sub_operations.operation_name: Counterbore/spotface 4× insert-nut recesses to 17.50 mm depth
sub_operations.reason: Create flush-or-below bracket-surface recesses for insert/nut installation before deburr and assembly.

tool.tool_description: Counterbore tool sized to confirmed insert/nut recess diameter
tool.notes: Diameter is not legible; must be confirmed.

sub_operations.notes: counterbore_diameter_mm is null due missing drawing value.

sub_operations.operation_name: Inspect M10 insert recess depth, boss pattern position and flushness allowance
sub_operations.reason: Insert-nut retention and flushness requirements depend on correct recess depth and boss position.

tool.tool_description: Depth gauge and position fixture/gauge

setups.inspection_at_setup: Inspect recess depth, pattern position and condition before deburring/insert operations.
setups.setup_reason: Dedicated SPM is justified by high-volume repeated boss pattern and stable A/B/C datums.

setups.setup_name: Deburr, edge break, clean and mark
setups.machine_reason: Manual finish bench is listed for deburr, edge break, cleaning and marking; this is required after machining and before insert installation/final inspection.

workholding.grip_description: Support on soft pads; protect datum pads and finished bores during hand finishing.
workholding.fixture_notes: Use bore plugs/protective sleeves during deburr if needed.

setups.stock_state_before: Fully machined housing before finish cleaning and marking.
setups.stock_state_after: Deburred, edge-broken, cleaned and marked machined housing ready for insert installation.

sub_operations.operation_name: Break all sharp edges 0.5 mm max and deburr hole/slot exits
sub_operations.reason: Drawing notes require sharp-edge removal and burr-free condition; surface treatment route also requires edge break before coating/assembly.

tool.tool_description: Hand deburr tools, scrapers, abrasive pads, back-deburr tools
tool.notes: Protect datum bores and pads.

sub_operations.operation_name: Clean housing to remove chips, abrasive residue and oils before insert installation
sub_operations.reason: Cleanliness is required before assembly, coating-related handling and final inspection.

tool.tool_description: Aqueous clean/air blow and lint-free wipe

sub_operations.operation_name: Mark part number/customer part/date code/cavity number per 3 mm character height and 0.3 mm raised/indent note
sub_operations.reason: Drawing general note requires identification marking before final inspection.

tool.tool_description: Dot-peen/indent marker or casting mark verification per customer note
tool.notes: If marking is cast-in raised lettering, verify rather than re-mark.

sub_operations.notes: Exact marked surface should be confirmed from drawing/model.

setups.inspection_at_setup: Visual burr and edge-break check before insert installation.
setups.setup_reason: Finish bench operations must occur before assembly/testing and before final inspection.

setups.setup_name: Zn-Ni coat or receive coated insert nuts
setups.machine_reason: Shop profile includes an in-house plating line. The drawing note calls for Zn-Ni coated insert nuts; confirm whether the in-house line supports the exact Zn-Ni chemistry or whether inserts are purchased pre-coated.

workholding.method: rack/barrel plating or incoming coated insert lot
workholding.grip_description: Handle four insert nuts per housing lot; protect internal threads so M10×1.5-6H gauges after coating.
workholding.fixture_notes: Use witness coupons and thread gauges per coating specification.

setups.stock_state_before: Deburred machined housings waiting; insert nuts uncoated or incoming coated lot pending verification.
setups.stock_state_after: Zn-Ni coated M10 insert nuts accepted for installation; machined housing remains clean and staged.

sub_operations.operation_name: Apply/verify Zn-Ni coating on 4× M10 insert nuts and verify 6H thread after coating
sub_operations.reason: Surface-treatment sequence places coating after component machining and before insert installation; thread class is specified after coating.

tool.tool_description: Plating rack/barrel and M10×1.5 6H go/no-go plug gauge
tool.notes: Confirm Zn-Ni availability; alkaline zinc line may need vendor support for Zn-Ni chemistry.

sub_operations.notes: If inserts are purchased already Zn-Ni coated, this setup becomes incoming inspection rather than plating.

setups.inspection_at_setup: Coating visual/thickness as specified and M10 go/no-go thread gauge after coating.
setups.setup_reason: Coated insert nuts must be ready before installation; coating after installation would risk housing masking and thread fit issues.

setups.machine_reason: The shop profile lists assembly and insert installation capability; the drawing includes nut push-out/anti-rotate and flushness requirements.

workholding.grip_description: Support housing under each boss/recess; press or stake insert nuts squarely without loading datum bores.
workholding.fixture_notes: Fixture must prevent casting distortion and verify nuts do not protrude beyond bracket surface.

setups.stock_state_before: Clean machined housing and accepted Zn-Ni coated insert nuts.
setups.stock_state_after: Housing assembly with four installed coated M10 insert nuts flush or below bracket surface.

sub_operations.operation_name: Install/press/stake 4× Zn-Ni coated M10×1.5-6H insert nuts into 17.50 mm recesses
sub_operations.reason: The final functional M10 threads are provided/verified in the insert nuts; installation follows machining, deburr, cleaning and coating verification.

tool.tool_description: Insertion/staking press fixture with flushness stop
tool.notes: Confirm insert nut type before tooling.

sub_operations.notes: If drawing actually requires direct tapped holes rather than insert nuts, replace this step with post-coating tapping/thread gauging.

setups.inspection_at_setup: Flush/below-surface visual and depth check immediately after installation.
setups.setup_reason: Nut installation must occur after machining, deburr/cleaning and insert coating verification.

setups.machine_reason: The shop profile includes R&D/quality testing capability; the drawing includes nut push-out, anti-rotate torque, and bracket validation test requirements.

workholding.grip_description: Use customer/control-plan test fixtures supporting datum features and loading indicated regions without damaging production measurement surfaces.
workholding.fixture_notes: Some durability/extreme tests may be sample/destructive rather than 100% production tests.

setups.stock_state_before: Machined, marked housing with installed coated insert nuts.
setups.stock_state_after: Tested housing or sampled lot with recorded nut retention/torque and validation results per control plan.

sub_operations.operation_name: Perform nut push-out, nut anti-rotate torque and required bracket validation tests per control plan
sub_operations.reason: Drawing requires 2 kN minimum nut push-out, 80 Nm anti-rotate torque, and bracket durability/extreme load testing requirements.

tool.tool_description: Push-out fixture, torque fixture, and bracket load fixtures per control plan
tool.notes: Test force/torque values are in notes but formula schema has no force/torque fields.

sub_operations.notes: Clarify which tests are 100% production, sample, destructive, or validation-only.

setups.inspection_at_setup: Record push-out/torque and control-plan test results.
setups.setup_reason: Functional tests are explicitly called out on the drawing and occur after insert installation.

setups.machine_reason: Shop profile includes mechanical standard-room inspection; production inspection can use gauges, bore gauges, height gauges and dedicated fixtures. External CMM FAI may be needed for launch.

workholding.grip_description: Locate on A pads and B/C/D bore gauges or pins; avoid clamping deformation.
workholding.fixture_notes: Tool room should support dedicated checking fixture for 1000-piece production; use external CMM for FAI if required.

setups.stock_state_before: Finished, cleaned, marked, insert-installed and tested housing.
setups.stock_state_after: Accepted finished housing ready for packaging/shipment after final inspection records.

sub_operations.operation_name: Final dimensional, thread, coating/insert, profile/position and visual inspection
sub_operations.reason: Final inspection follows deburr, cleaning, marking and testing, and verifies all critical datums, holes, profile controls, insert flushness and thread condition.

tool.tool_description: Bore gauges, pin gauges, M10 plug gauges, height gauge, profile fixture; external CMM for FAI if required
tool.notes: CMM not listed in-house.

sub_operations.notes: Final CMM FAI is recommended because profile/position GD&T cannot be fully verified from basic gauges alone.

setups.inspection_at_setup: Final inspection report; external CMM FAI recommended for launch/sample approval.
setups.setup_reason: Inspection is last after deburr, clean, mark, insert installation and testing.

routing_notes: Saw-cut stock is skipped because the selected stock is a near-net casting, per the milling/casting route rules.

CNC lathe is intentionally not used: although there are coaxial bores/steps, the overall housing is a complex casting, not a round-bar/axisymmetric workpiece, and the VMC can finish the coaxial features by boring/circular interpolation.

Rough machining precedes final finishing to respect casting distortion/stress redistribution rules. Datum A is rough-established first, all major features are roughed, then A/B/C/D and datum-referenced features are finished.
Hole-making rules are applied: spot before drill, final drill/ream in the same setup for precision bores, larger/coaxial clevis features before smaller holes, and deburr after through holes/slots.

The plan treats M10 threads as installed Zn-Ni coated insert nuts because the notes mention insert nuts, push-out force and anti-rotate torque. If the housing itself must be tapped, add a post-coating M10 tapping/thread-gauge operation after clarification.
All setup_time_min values are set to 0.0 intentionally; no setup-time or cycle-time estimates are calculated in this planning output.

clarifications.question: Confirm the material grade, casting process, and whether any stress relief/aging/heat treatment is required before machining.
clarifications.why_it_matters: Material drives casting supplier selection, machining parameters, coating compatibility, and whether the casting-route stress-relief step is mandatory.
clarifications.suggested_default: Proceed provisionally as a machinable automotive casting alloy with foundry-supplied stabilization if required.

clarifications.question: Confirm the general tolerance standard/default title-block tolerances.
clarifications.why_it_matters: Many dimensions are untoleranced or marked O/R/I/P; default tolerances determine whether VMC/SPM operations are sufficient or whether extra finishing/inspection is needed.

clarifications.question: Confirm whether the 4× M10×1.5-6H features are installed insert nuts or direct tapped holes in the housing; also confirm thread depth, insert-hole diameter, and counterbore/recess diameter for the 17.50 mm recess.
clarifications.why_it_matters: This determines whether the route uses insert installation only or adds a tapping/thread-milling operation, and it affects formula inputs for drilling/counterboring/assembly.
clarifications.suggested_default: Treat as Zn-Ni coated M10 insert nuts installed into machined recesses, with 6H thread verified after coating.

clarifications.question: Provide verified local through-depths/wall thicknesses for Ø16.90 datum bores, Ø72.04/Ø70 central bores, Ø30.60/Ø20.10/Ø10.50 clevis holes, and the clevis slots.
clarifications.why_it_matters: Several hole_depth_mm and slot_length_mm values are missing or inferred from envelope values; accurate depths are required for tool length, peck cycles, and cycle-time formulas.
clarifications.suggested_default: Use extracted through-depths only for provisional routing; use 3D model before programming.

clarifications.question: Provide the casting/foundry drawing or 3D model stock map, including cored bore diameters and machining allowance distribution.
clarifications.why_it_matters: bore_start_diameter_mm and roughing stock are unknown for the central bore train and cast profiles; this affects rough-boring and rough-milling inputs.

clarifications.question: Confirm exact GD&T feature-control-frame values and datum references for M10 pattern, B/C bores, datum D bore, central steps, clevis holes, clevis slots and exterior perimeter profile.
clarifications.why_it_matters: Small raster FCF values drive inspection method, fixture datum scheme, and whether external CMM FAI is mandatory.
clarifications.suggested_default: Use extracted values provisionally with lower confidence.

clarifications.question: Confirm coating scope and specification: are only insert nuts Zn-Ni coated, or is any housing surface coated? Confirm coating thickness and whether Almity's plating line supports the exact Zn-Ni process.
clarifications.why_it_matters: Coating sequence, masking, thread gauging, and outside-vendor need depend on coating scope and chemistry.
clarifications.suggested_default: Assume only insert nuts receive Zn-Ni coating and are gauged M10×1.5-6H after coating.

clarifications.question: Confirm whether irregular pockets/windows, radial ribs, exterior profile and marking are fully cast/model-controlled or require additional machining not visible in the raster drawing.
clarifications.why_it_matters: F021/F022/F026 and marking are not fully dimensioned; the route currently treats them as cast/model-controlled with only controlled profile cleanup where required.
clarifications.suggested_default: Treat as cast/model-controlled geometry unless the 3D model calls for machining.

assumptions.text: Material is missing; provisional process assumes a machinable automotive casting alloy and no unlisted hardening operation beyond foundry-required stabilization.
Near-net casting is selected because the feature graph describes a complex cast housing with ribs, lightening pockets, bosses and clevis lugs, and the production quantity is 1000 pieces.
Where a feature is marked THRU but local depth is not extracted, the route leaves formula hole_depth_mm null or uses the extracted 143.6 mm value only when the feature graph explicitly provides it.
The M10×1.5-6H features are treated as Zn-Ni coated insert nuts installed into machined recesses; no direct tapping operation is planned until confirmed.
Cored starting diameters and casting stock allowances are not provided; central bore roughing/finishing uses final bore diameters with bore_start_diameter_mm left null where unknown.

Ribs, lightening pockets, exterior cast contours and cast radii are primarily produced by the casting/model; machining is limited to datum, functional, controlled and cleanup surfaces called out by the drawing/model.
setup_time_min is set to 0.0 for every setup because this output does not calculate setup time, cycle time, feed, speed or cost.

analysis_trace.summary: The feature graph describes a complex 3D cast housing/bracket with datum target pads A1-A4, B/C locating bores, a central D bore/annular coaxial stack, clevis slots/holes, ribs, pockets, controlled perimeter and M10 insert-nut features. The material and several depths are missing.
issues: M10 insert versus direct tapped-hole interpretation is uncertain.
Several depths, counterbore diameter and model-controlled profiles are missing.

analysis_trace.summary: Retrieved route sequences for casting, milling, hole_making, surface_treatment and turning. Casting, milling, hole_making and surface_treatment are applicable. Turning was reviewed but rejected because the part is not primarily cylindrical and cannot be practically lathe-held as a rotational workpiece.
issues: Casting is outside/vendor because no foundry is listed.
Surface treatment appears to apply to insert nuts only, not the full housing.

analysis_trace.summary: Classified the part as hybrid with complex base geometry: near-net casting plus VMC/SPM machining, insert/coating handling, testing and inspection.
issues: Material class for correction search was assumed as aluminium-like automotive casting due missing material and insert-nut housing context.

analysis_trace.summary: Called organizational routing corrections for hybrid/complex casting-like housing with internal bores, holes, slots, threads, coating and volume 1000. No organization-specific corrections were returned.
issues: No corrective routing lessons available for this exact case.

analysis_trace.summary: Selected outsourced near-net casting rather than bar/block because the geometry has variable walls, ribs, pockets, bosses and clevis lugs, and quantity 1000 justifies casting tooling. Estimated stock envelope uses 3 mm machining allowance per side on the finished envelope.
issues: Actual casting allowance and material must be confirmed by foundry drawing/model.

analysis_trace.summary: Composed a route with foundry casting/stabilization, rough datum A, rough all major features, finish datum A, finish B/C/D and central bore stack, finish clevis/perimeter, SPM finish M10 insert recesses, deburr/clean/mark, coat/receive insert nuts, install inserts, test and inspect.
issues: CMM FAI may be external because in-house CMM is not listed.
Exact M10 workflow may change if features are direct tapped holes rather than inserts.

analysis_trace.summary: Decomposed each setup into machining/assembly/test/inspection sub-operations, using milling, hole-making, casting and surface-treatment formula families and populating available geometric inputs from feature graph dimensions.
issues: Several formula inputs are null because dimensions are not present: counterbore diameter, thread depth, bore start diameters, slot length and several local hole depths.

analysis_trace.summary: Feature coverage includes every non-part-body feature F002-F029 through casting, machining, assembly, testing or inspection. Key rules are satisfied: datum surfaces first, rough before finish for casting, spot before drilling, final drill/ream in same setup, deburr/clean/mark before final inspection, and coating/insert handling before assembly/testing.
issues: Feature coverage for cast/model-controlled ribs and pockets is via casting and final inspection rather than machining.
Final inspection should include external CMM FAI or dedicated gauges for GD&T-controlled features.
